title: Causal and Noncausal Systems
summary: This page discusses memoryless versus dynamic systems, explaining that memoryless systems depend only on the current input while dynamic systems have memory of past inputs. Example 1.12 analyzes three systems to determine if they are memoryless, including a time-shifted system, a derivative system, and a time-varying coefficient system. The page then introduces causal (nonanticipative) systems, which depend only on past and present input values.
